# Docs-Only Repository Example

This example demonstrates a documentation-only workflow using `ai-workflow`.

**Context**: Adding API documentation to an existing project.

## Workflow Summary

| Step | File | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| Requirement | `01-requirement.md` | Define documentation scope |
| Specification | `02-functional-spec.md` | Define docs structure and content |
| Tech Plan | `03-technical-plan.md` | Define file locations and format |
| PR Breakdown | `04-pr-breakdown.md` | Split into reviewable PRs |
| Handoff | `05-execution-handoff.md` | Implementation context |
| Validation | `06-validation-report.md` | Evidence of completion |

## When to use this example

Use this when the change is purely documentation:
- README updates
- API documentation
- Runbook creation
- Template changes

The workflow skips implementation steps and focuses on clarity and accuracy.
